verses.text: In distress the psalmist cried to the LORD and was heard, praying to be delivered 'from lying lips, and from a deceitful tongue.' False speech surrounds him. The believer takes even the wounds of slander to God.

In distress the psalmist cried to the LORD and was heard, praying to be delivered 'from lying lips, and from a deceitful tongue.' False speech surrounds him. The believer takes even the wounds of slander to God.
He asks what the false tongue shall earn — 'sharp arrows... with coals of juniper,' piercing and lasting punishment. Lies will be repaid. The deceitful tongue does not escape the judgment of God.
He asks what the false tongue shall earn — 'sharp arrows... with coals of juniper,' piercing and lasting punishment. Lies will be repaid. The deceitful tongue does not escape the judgment of God.

He grieves to dwell among the hostile, like living in Meshech or Kedar: 'I am for peace, but... they are for war.' The peaceable suffer among the contentious. The godly often long for peace in a world bent on strife.
